arts.ttl: Why is Arne Slot on the touchline for Aston Villa vs Liverpool despite 'two game ban'
arts.sum: Arne Slot may have been given a red card in the fiery Merseyside Derby clash against Everton, but the Liverpool manager's fate is yet to be decided by the Premier League

arts.ttl: Bernardo Silva makes shock Liverpool Premier League title admission as 'difficult' position explained
arts.sum: Liverpool are currently 16 points clear of defending Premier League champions Manchester City

arts.ttl: Aston Villa vs Liverpool referee changed on late notice as new official steps in
arts.sum: Stuart Attwell was initially supposed to officiate Aston Villa's Premier League clash with Liverpool on Wednesday evening, but Craig Pawson will now take on the match duties instead

arts.ttl: Aston Villa must bring their fantastic cup form to flawed league campaign
arts.sum: Unai Emery’s team need new signings to shine as they seek statement win over Liverpool in the Premier League “How is the season going: good or bad?” Unai Emery said, posing a question many Aston Villa supporters have been asking themselves. It is one with no simple answer. “It depends, if we are watching Champions League, wow, fantastic,” the Villa manager said, applauding like an enthusiastic theatregoer, dusting off his hands at qualifying automatically for the last 16. “If we are watching the FA Cup, fantastic, after a long time we are playing, close to progress,” he said of the club reaching the fifth round for the first time in 10 years. Then came an aching, juddering groan. “If we are watching the Premier League, errr, it’s not enough.” The other thing Emery knows is this is a significant week for Villa, who host Liverpool on Wednesday night and Chelsea on Saturday hoping to breathe life into their quest to qualify for the Champions League in successive seasons, with fifth spot likely to offer a route on to that stage given English clubs’ success in Europe this term. Villa visit Crystal Palace next Tuesday and the following Friday they entertain Cardiff City, at risk of relegation to League One, for a place in the Cup quarter-finals. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Liverpool XI vs Aston Villa – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Liverpool travel to Aston Villa on Wednesday night looking to extend their lead at the top of the Premier League to 10 points. Arne Slot’s side overcame Wolves at the weekend, though a flat second-half performance will be a concern. The club’s clash at Villa has been rescheduled for midweek, due to Liverpool’s involvement in […] The post Liverpool XI vs Aston Villa – Predicted lineup and team news first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.sum: Every day we'll challenge you to name a different starting XI from the Premier League era. We'll give you a team's rough formation, and the number of letters in each player's name. Then it's up to you!

arts.ttl: Aston Villa vs. Liverpool: How can Arne Slot get the better of Unai Emery to continue Premier League title charge
arts.sum: Liverpool are set to take on Aston Villa away from home in the Premier League and could move 10 points ahead of Arsenal with a victory.

arts.ttl: Arne Slot says Liverpool player with just eight PL starts this season is in line for 'a lot of minutes' from now on
arts.sum: Liverpool remain top of the Premier League table by seven points after they won their weekend game. Arne Slot’s Reds ran out 2-1 winners over...

arts.ttl: Unai Emery makes 'not enough' Aston Villa admission as Champions League hopes diminish
arts.sum: Unai Emery admits he is frustrated by Aston Villa’s lack of consistency in the Premier League, but acknowledges the club is in a process and qualifying for the Champions League in successive seasons was always going to be a huge ask. "It is always about getting balance and trying to identify how we are in each moment," Emery said ahead of Villa’s Premier League clash against leaders Liverpool.
